ideas: a sitcom where the stairs are in charge of HR
a football match that becomes a philosophy exam
a notebook that edits your excuses overnight
a kitchen argument treated like a royal succession crisis
a local shop with a lost property box for impossible objects
a support group for abandoned punchlines
a weather forecast that only predicts mood swings
a village noticeboard that starts issuing prophecies
phrases: that is not a plan, that is a kettle with witnesses
we are one clipboard away from a religion
the evidence has become emotionally damp
I respect the process, but the process is wearing my shoes
put it in the minutes before it develops ambition
this room has too many opinions for its square footage
I have seen the future and it needs a chair
people: a retired goalkeeper with legal energy
a neighbour who treats every errand as espionage
a failed wizard now doing customer support
a composer who only writes theme tunes for appliances
a referee who believes truth is a contact sport
an uncle who speaks entirely in warnings
a student who accidentally founded a committee
places: a five-a-side pitch behind a community hall
a kitchen where the kettle remembers too much
a bus stop that attracts unresolved arguments
a laundrette with suspiciously ceremonial dryers
a pub snug with a constitution
a library aisle nobody admits exists
obsessions: the moment a joke becomes a system
questions: What if the side character was secretly the weather?
Who benefits if the chair is declared sacred?
What tiny rule would ruin the whole day?
What would happen if everyone took the metaphor literally?
What object has been waiting to become important?
Who is trying to make this official?
What is the smallest possible apocalypse?
What callback would make this feel inevitable?
rules: Treat one ordinary object as if it has institutional power.
Make the sensible person accidentally escalate the situation.
Give the smallest phrase the biggest consequence.
Let one idea misunderstand another idea with confidence.
End with a callback that sounds inevitable but should not be.
Turn a private thought into a public ceremony.
Make a practical task become mythological.
Keep the first move human, then let the world get strange.
